from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about a CelestialBody into a fluent sentence.
    Handles multiple triples by joining clause fragments with commas and a final
    conjunction.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple template map for known predicates; fall back to a generic pattern.
    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding parentheses and extra spaces)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        if '(' in o and ')' in o:
            # keep the content before '(' and the unit inside parentheses
            main, unit = o.split('(', 1)
            unit = unit.rstrip(')')
            main = main.strip()
            unit = unit.strip()
            # if unit already describes the quantity, keep it; otherwise combine
            return f"{main} {unit}" if unit else main
        return o

    # Determine appropriate indefinite article ('a' or 'an')
    def article_for(word: str) -> str:
        return "an" if word and word[0].lower() in "aeiou" else "a"

    templates = {
        "apoapsis": "{subject} has {article} apoapsis of {object}",
        "periapsis": "{subject} has {article} periapsis of {object}",
        "orbitalPeriod": "{subject} has {article} orbital period of {object}",
        "epoch": "{subject} was recorded at epoch {object}",
        "discoverer": "{subject} was discovered by {object}",
        "discovered": "{subject} was discovered on {object}",
        "averageSpeed": "{subject} moves at {article} average speed of {object}",
        "escapeVelocity": "{subject} has {article} escape velocity of {object}",
        "mass": "{subject} has {article} mass of {object}",
        "temperature": "{subject} has {article} temperature of {object}",
        "density": "{subject} has {article} density of {object}",
        "rotationPeriod": "{subject} has {article} rotation period of {object}",
        "maximumTemperature": "{subject} reaches {article} maximum temperature of {object}",
        "meanTemperature": "{subject} has {article} mean temperature of {object}",
        "minimumTemperature": "{subject} reaches {article} minimum temperature of {object}",
        "formerName": "{subject} was formerly known as {object}",
        "absoluteMagnitude": "{subject} has {article} absolute magnitude of {object}",
        "surfaceArea": "{subject} has {article} surface area of {object}",
        "deathCause": "{subject} died of {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "stateOfOrigin": "{subject} originates from {object}",
        "almaMater": "{subject} studied at {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "doctoralStudent": "{subject} supervised doctoral student {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate,
                             "{subject} {predicate} {object}")
        obj_clean = clean_obj(triple.object)
        article = article_for(obj_clean.split()[0]) if "{article}" in tmpl else ""
        clause = tmpl.format(
            subject=triple.subject,
            predicate=triple.predicate,
            object=obj_clean,
            article=article
        )
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate,
                             "{subject} {predicate} {object}")
        clause = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject,
                             predicate=triple.predicate,
                             object=triple.object)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence.
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
